title: 控方证人
summary:
On a stormy December night, Seleras Hammel and Dick Borrow walk home from a dinner party hosted by psychiatrist Bernard Seldon. The two men represent opposing philosophies: Borrow is a lean, ascetic man driven by illusions and physical misery, while Hammel is a wealthy, self-satisfied materialist who rose from being a newsboy to seek the luxury money provides.

After parting ways at a metro station, Hammel walks alone in the cold. While waiting to cross a street, he witnesses a horrific accident where a bus strikes a man, leaving a pile of lifeless limbs in the road. Terrified by the suddenness of death, Hammel experiences an intense, uncontrollable fear. Seeking to escape his anxiety, he takes a shortcut through a narrow alley toward his home near the square.

In the dim alley, Hammel encounters a legless street performer playing a flute. The music is a strange, rising, and uplifting fragment that resembles a melody from "Rienzi." The experience is so profound that Hammel feels a ridiculous illusion of floating. He pursues the man to ask about the music. The performer is a young man with a beautiful face and a clear, non-English accent who claims to have come from "the other side of the sea" a very long time ago. When Hammel expresses sympathy for his lost legs, the man solemnly replies that his legs "were evil." Hammel gives him a shilling and returns home, unsettled by the encounter. As he lies in bed, he hears a faint, familiar sound that reminds him of the flute music, causing his heart to race.
